status: exact thin-collar Wronskian reduction of corrected critical C1 transversality; open arithmetic separation, not Lambda<=0 or RH
proof_boundary: This artifact proves exact complex, polar, Wronskian, and robust collision identities. The numerical rows only reject an unjustified one-sided phase-sign shortcut. It does not prove the L>=50 thin-collar Wronskian target on either the live asymptotic layer or the bounded-L residual domains, corrected transversality, Lambda<=0, or RH.

exact.complex_split: Choose the sharp endpoint half q_(N,t) so Q_(N,t)=2*Re(q_(N,t)) on the real axis, and set E_(N,t)=exp(i*beta_t)D_(N,t)-q_(N,t); then J_(N,t)=2*Re(E_(N,t)) and J_(N,t)'=2*Re(E_(N,t)')
exact.polar: If E=a*exp(i*theta), u=(log(a))', and v=theta', then T_L[J]=4*a^2*(cos(theta)^2+(u*cos(theta)-v*sin(theta))^2/L^2) and W_E=a^2*v
exact.crossing: At Re(E)=0 with E!=0, T_L[J]=4*W_E^2/(|E|^2*L^2); J=J'=0 if and only if theta'=0
exact.thin_collar_target: For every critical point, either |Re(E)|>epsilon_0/2 or 2*|W_E|>epsilon_0*|E'|+L*epsilon_1*|E|
exact.target_domains: The explicit formulas remain valid on the original domain L>=50, 0<tL<=25. After the oscillatory-zeta theorem, the live asymptotic high-frequency layer is 0<tL<=c_*+o(1), with c_*=4911678521/1933561194; uncovered bounded L remains a separate compact-certificate obligation
exact.explicit_target: With epsilon_0=2500*exp(-3L/4) and epsilon_1=5000*exp(-3L/4), either |Re(E)|>1250*exp(-3L/4) or 2*|W_E|>exp(-3L/4)*(2500*|E'|+5000*L*|E|)
exact.critical_value_form: Where E!=0, collision means theta is pi/2 modulo pi and theta'=0; the open arithmetic theorem is quantitative avoidance of those phase critical levels
exact.generator_bridge: At J=0 with E=iY and Y!=0, W_E=-Y*J'/2; along a moving simple zero x_*(t), x_*'=-J_t/J'=J_t*Y/(2*W_E)
exact.pair_wronskian: If J(x)=(x-alpha)*(x-beta)*G(x), then |W_E(alpha)|=|E(alpha)|*(beta-alpha)*|G(alpha)|/2; the Wronskian margin is linearly close-pair sensitive

diagnostics.proof_boundary: Moderate-frequency t=0 points show that neither a global nor a crossing-only one-sided phase sign follows formally. They do not establish the L>=50 thin-collar target.

rows.claim: The corrected real main is the real part of one explicit complex main.
rows.formula: Choose the sharp endpoint half q_(N,t) so Q_(N,t)=2*Re(q_(N,t)) on the real axis, and set E_(N,t)=exp(i*beta_t)D_(N,t)-q_(N,t); then J_(N,t)=2*Re(E_(N,t)) and J_(N,t)'=2*Re(E_(N,t)')
rows.proof_boundary: Real axis with one fixed analytic cutoff lift.

rows.claim: The corrected C1 target is the Euclidean first jet of the real part of E.
Away from zeros of E, the first jet separates radial and phase motion exactly.
rows.formula: If E=a*exp(i*theta), u=(log(a))', and v=theta', then T_L[J]=4*a^2*(cos(theta)^2+(u*cos(theta)-v*sin(theta))^2/L^2) and W_E=a^2*v
rows.proof_boundary: Only the polar rewrite assumes E is nonzero.

rows.claim: At a corrected crossing, simplicity is exactly nonvanishing phase velocity.
rows.formula: W_E=Im(E'*conj(E))=V*X-U*Y; At Re(E)=0 with E!=0, T_L[J]=4*W_E^2/(|E|^2*L^2); J=J'=0 if and only if theta'=0

rows.claim: A true double zero forces an explicit upper bound on the complex Wronskian.
rows.proof_boundary: Uses only the certified normalized C1 remainder caps.

rows.claim: It is sufficient to prove Wronskian separation only in the exponentially thin corrected-value collar.
rows.formula: For every critical point, either |Re(E)|>epsilon_0/2 or 2*|W_E|>epsilon_0*|E'|+L*epsilon_1*|E|

rows.claim: The global remainder constants give a concrete crossing-localized arithmetic target.
rows.formula: With epsilon_0=2500*exp(-3L/4) and epsilon_1=5000*exp(-3L/4), either |Re(E)|>1250*exp(-3L/4) or 2*|W_E|>exp(-3L/4)*(2500*|E'|+5000*L*|E|)
rows.proof_boundary: Open on the original L>=50, 0<tL<=25 domain. Asymptotically the live high-frequency layer is 0<tL<=c_*+o(1); bounded-L residuals remain separate compact obligations.

rows.claim: The surviving phase-critical obstruction is exactly the collapsing denominator of the zero generator and is linearly close-pair sensitive.
rows.formula: Where E!=0, collision means theta is pi/2 modulo pi and theta'=0; the open arithmetic theorem is quantitative avoidance of those phase critical levels; At J=0 with E=iY and Y!=0, W_E=-Y*J'/2; along a moving simple zero x_*(t), x_*'=-J_t/J'=J_t*Y/(2*W_E); If J(x)=(x-alpha)*(x-beta)*G(x), then |W_E(alpha)|=|E(alpha)|*(beta-alpha)*|G(alpha)|/2; the Wronskian margin is linearly close-pair sensitive

rows.claim: Finite corrected examples have both phase signs, including at corrected crossings.
rows.formula: one-sided phase sign is not an algebraic consequence of the corrected representation

rows.claim: The Wronskian reduction and finite crossing diagnostics do not prove phase-level avoidance.
rows.formula: exact reduction plus finite diagnostics != corrected transversality
